mapping.a_element: critical points in accretion flow (saddle and centre-type points)
mapping.b_element: quasinormal modes (QNMs) of the acoustic black hole

mapping.a_element: dynamical systems analysis of critical points to classify stability
mapping.b_element: numerical analysis of quasinormal frequencies to assess stability

mapping.a_element: perturbation equation governing stability under radial perturbations
mapping.b_element: imaginary part of quasinormal frequencies indicating stability against small perturbations
rotating draining bathtub metric as the acoustic black hole analogue

mapping.a_element: upper bound on angular momentum for critical solutions
mapping.b_element: rotation parameter B in the acoustic black hole metric

brief_justification: Both papers analyze the stability of stationary solutions in analogue black hole systems using perturbation theory: Paper A via dynamical systems analysis of critical points in accretion flows, and Paper B via quasinormal mode frequencies in acoustic black holes. The perturbation equations and stability criteria (absence of growing modes) play analogous roles, with the pseudo-potential and acoustic metric serving as the governing frameworks for their respective systems.
